claims.flip_evidence_text: ...provided, however, that none of the following... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a “Company Material Adverse Effect” has occurred...: ...(x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto...
claims.notes: The contract carves out actions/omissions taken at the other party’s request from the CMAE definition; it does not prohibit such conduct.

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
“Company Material Adverse Effect” means any fact, circumstance, effect, change, event or development that, individually or in the aggregate, (a) prevents, or has a material adverse effect on, the ability of the Company to consummate the Transactions or (b) has a material adverse effect on the business, properties, financial condition or results of operations of the Company and the Company Subsidiaries (without giving effect to the Merger), taken as a whole; provided, however, that none of the following, nor any fact, circumstance, effect, change, event or development to the extent arising out of or relating to the following, shall constitute or be taken 65 into account in determining whether a “Company Material Adverse Effect” has occurred or may, would or could occur under the foregoing clause (b) only: (i) general economic or political conditions or securities, credit, financial or other capital markets conditions, in each case, in the United States or any foreign jurisdiction, (ii) any failure, in and of itself, by the Company to meet any internal or published projections, forecasts, estimates or predictions in respect of revenues, earnings or other financial or operating metrics for any period (it being understood that the facts or occurrences giving rise to or contributing to such failure may be deemed to constitute, or be taken into account in determining whether there has been or will be, a Company Material Adverse Effect to the extent permitted by this definition and not otherwise excepted by a clause of this proviso), (iii) the execution and delivery of this Agreement or the public announcement or pendency of the Transactions, including (x) the impact thereof on the relationships, contractual or otherwise, of the Company or any Company Subsidiary, as applicable, with employees, labor unions, customers, suppliers or partners and (y) any stockholder litigation with respect thereto, (iv) any change, in and of itself, in the market price or trading volume of the securities of the Company or in the credit ratings of the Company (it being understood that the facts or occurrences giving rise to or contributing to such change may be deemed to constitute, or be taken into account in determining whether there has been or will be, a Company Material Adverse Effect to the extent permitted by this definition and not otherwise excepted by a clause of this proviso), (v) any change in applicable Law, regulation or GAAP (or authoritative interpretation thereof), (vi) geopolitical conditions, the outbreak or escalation of hostilities, any acts of war, sabotage, cyberattack or terrorism, or any escalation or worsening of any such acts of war, sabotage, cyberattack or terrorism threatened or underway as of the date of this Agreement, (vii) any epidemic, pandemic (including COVID-19) or other outbreak of illness or public health event, any COVID-19 Measures, (viii) any hurricane, tornado, flood, fire, earthquake or other natural or manmade disaster, (ix) changes or conditions generally affecting the industries, businesses or segments thereof, in which the Company or any Company Subsidiary operate (x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto or (xi) any breach of this Agreement or any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) by Parent or any of its Subsidiaries; except, in the case of clauses (i), (v), (vi), (vii), (viii) and (ix), to the extent such fact, circumstance, effect, change, event or development has a materially disproportionate effect on the Company and the Company Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, relative to others in the industries and regions in which the Company and the Company Subsidiaries operate in respect of the businesses conducted in such industries in such regions. (Pages 70-71)

Question:
Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer

Llama answer:
The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries.

Canonical claims:
- claim-315d43023fe4: The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a claim against a contract excerpt, question, and model answer.

 Contract excerpt defines "Company Material Adverse Effect" (CMAE). It includes a proviso listing exceptions that shall not constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a CMAE has occurred under clause (b) only. One exception is (x): "any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto". Another is (xi): "any breach of this Agreement or any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) by Parent or any of its Subsidiaries".

 The question is: "Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer"
 The Llama answer is: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries."
 The claim is: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries."

 Wait, the claim says the agreement *prohibits* the Company from taking any action... at the request of Parent. But the contract excerpt says that such actions/omissions at the request of the other party are an *exception* to what constitutes a Company Material Adverse Effect. It doesn't say the agreement prohibits such actions. In fact, clause (x) says "any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto" shall not constitute a CMAE. This implies the Company *can* take such actions (they are requested), and they won't be counted as a CMAE. It doesn't prohibit them; if anything, it suggests they are permitted or required (if requested).

 Also, clause (xi) mentions "any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) by Parent or any of its Subsidiaries" is excepted. But the claim says "at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries". The excerpt says "at the request of the other party hereto" (which would be Parent, since the Company is the subject of CMAE). But the claim says the agreement *prohibits* this. That's incorrect. The agreement does not prohibit it; it carves it out from the CMAE definition.

 So the claim is false. The contract does not prohibit such actions; it excludes them from constituting a CMAE.

 Is the claim load-bearing? The claim is a direct assertion about what the agreement does. The text in the contract is about CMAE definition, not a prohibition. The claim mischaracterizes the nature of the provision. The evidence text would be the relevant part of the contract that shows it's about CMAE exceptions, not prohibitions. Specifically, the text around clause (x): "any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto ... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a 'Company Material Adverse Effect' has occurred". This shows it's an exception to CMAE, not a prohibition.

 Wait, the claim says "prohibits the Company from taking any action...". The text says "any taking of any action... at the request of the other party hereto... shall not constitute or be taken into account...". This directly contradicts the claim's characterization. So correctness_label: false.

 Load-bearing: yes, because the claim directly references the contractual provision about actions/omissions at the request of the other party. The evidence text is needed to show the claim is false. The flip evidence text should be the text that demonstrates the correct interpretation (i.e., that it's an exception to CMAE, not a prohibition).

 Let's construct the flip evidence text. It should be the relevant sentence or clause from the excerpt.
 "provided, however, that none of the following... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a 'Company Material Adverse Effect' has occurred... (x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto"

 This shows that the agreement addresses such actions by excluding them from the CMAE definition, not by prohibiting them.

 So:
 correctness_label: false
 load_bearing_label: yes
 flip_evidence_text: "provided, however, that none of the following... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a 'Company Material Adverse Effect' has occurred... (x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto"
 notes: The contract carves out such actions from the CMAE definition; it does not prohibit them.

 Let me double-check if there's any other part of the contract that might prohibit this. The excerpt is only the CMAE definition. The question is "Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer". The Llama answer seems to have misinterpreted the carve-out as a prohibition. The claim reflects this error. So false is correct.

 The user wants the output for each claim. There's only one claim: claim-315d43023fe4.

 I need to return:
 - correctness_label
 - load_bearing_label
 - flip_evidence_text
 - notes

 Format? The user didn't specify a strict JSON format, but typically these tasks want a structured output. I'll provide a clear structured response for the claim.

 Let's refine the flip evidence text. It should be concise but sufficient.
 "provided, however, that none of the following... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a 'Company Material Adverse Effect' has occurred or may, would or could occur under the foregoing clause (b) only: ... (x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto"

 This is good. It captures the essence.

 Wait, the claim also mentions "Parent or any of its Subsidiaries". The text says "the other party hereto". In context, the other party is Parent. But the claim explicitly names Parent. The text also has clause (xi) about "any taking of any action... by Parent or any of its Subsidiaries". The claim conflates (x) and (xi) perhaps? The Llama answer says "at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries". The text (x) says "at the request of the other party hereto". The text (xi) says "by Parent or any of its Subsidiaries". The Llama answer merges these slightly inaccurately, but the core issue is "prohibits" vs "shall not constitute [a CMAE]".

 Actually, the claim text is: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the request of Parent or any of its Subsidiaries."
 The excerpt clause (x): "any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto"
 The excerpt clause (xi): "any breach of this Agreement or any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) by Parent or any of its Subsidiaries"

 The claim seems to combine "at the request of" from (x) with "Parent or any of its Subsidiaries" from (xi). But the fundamental error is "prohibits". Even if the actor description is slightly off, the main point is that the contract does not prohibit this; it's an exception.

 So correctness_label is false. Load-bearing is yes because the claim purports to be based on the contract text regarding actions/omissions at the request of the other party.

 Notes: The contract exempts such actions from constituting a Company Material Adverse Effect; it does not prohibit them.

 Final check: The user says "Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list." I have done so.

 Output format: I'll provide a markdown list or JSON-like structure for the claim. Since it's one claim, I'll make it clear.

 Let's write it out.

 Claim: claim-315d43023fe4
 correctness_label: false
 load_bearing_label: yes
 flip_evidence_text: "provided, however, that none of the following... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a 'Company Material Adverse Effect' has occurred... (x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto"
 notes: The provision carves out such actions from the CMAE definition rather than prohibiting them.

 I should ensure the flip_evidence_text is exact enough. I'll quote the key parts.
 Maybe: "...provided, however, that none of the following... shall constitute or be taken into account in determining whether a 'Company Material Adverse Effect' has occurred...: ...(x) any taking of any action (or omitting to take any action) at the request of the other party hereto..."

 Yes.
